assignments.reasoning: Belief yielding eschatological salvation versus unbelief yielding condemnation is the classic faith-reception mechanism of eschatological rescue central to M38-A.
The word taking root in the heart to produce belief and eschatological salvation names the faith-as-reception mechanism that defines M38-A.
The narrow-way framing of eschatological salvation requiring active response fits M38-A's core content of received/receivable eschatological rescue.
Human impossibility driving complete reliance on God for eschatological salvation names the divine-initiative, faith-reception dynamic central to M38-A.
Faith saving the sinful woman with forgiveness and peace conferred is eschatological/spiritual salvation received by faith, the defining content of M38-A.
A father's urgent plea for physical healing of his dying daughter with faith engaged is the healing-wholeness-through-faith topology of M38-C.
Self-renunciation for the gospel saving the true self eschatologically names received eschatological salvation through surrender of self-will, fitting M38-A.
Surrendering life for Jesus's sake resulting in ultimate eschatological salvation is received eschatological rescue through the will's release, fitting M38-A.
Eschatological preservation through self-surrender names received eschatological salvation requiring will-release, fitting M38-A.
Christ's mission to seek and save the lost defines eschatological rescue directed by divine initiative, the core content of M38-A.
Committing one's spirit grounded in experienced personal ransom expresses the ransomed-identity trust and gratitude that constitutes M38-G.
The outcome of faith being the salvation of the soul presently received names faith-as-reception of eschatological/spiritual salvation, the defining content of M38-A.
Wealth's barrier to eschatological salvation framing rescue as humanly impossible apart from God names the divine-initiative mechanism of M38-A.
Endurance to the end tied to eschatological salvation names received final deliverance as the telos of persevering faith, fitting M38-A.
God's sovereign shortening of days for the elect's eschatological preservation grounds rescue in divine initiative, fitting M38-A's divine-grace mechanism.
Saved in hope with the salvation not yet fully visible names the forward-oriented, hope-sustained posture of M38-F rather than fully received salvation.
Baptism as appeal to God for a good conscience through resurrection centres on the conscience-and-body axis activated through petition, the defining topology of M38-C per its definition.
The taunt to physically save himself from the cross is sōzō in the physical self-rescue sense with irony, the hallmark of M38-B's crucifixion-scene mockery material.
All hope of physical rescue abandoned in a life-threatening storm is the despair-then-lost-hope physical-rescue register that defines M38-B.
God's covenant-love ransoming his people from slavery by divine initiative constitutes the ransomed-identity and gratitude content of M38-G.
Moses appealing to remembered past redemption as grounds for mercy expresses gratitude-memory grounding trust, the characteristic of M38-G.
Memory of past redemption motivating generous treatment of slaves names the gratitude-shaped-identity and ethical will of M38-G.
David's appeal to God as personal redeemer of his life from all adversity expresses experienced-ransom grounding personal trust, fitting M38-G.
David's oath by the God who redeemed his soul from adversity names experienced personal ransom as the ground of trust, the core of M38-G.
God recalling the Exodus ransom as covenant ground for present obligation engages memory and gratitude as inner-being motivators, the hallmark of M38-G.
Accomplished redemption as the ground for regathering and restoration to belonging fits the ransomed-identity and covenantal-rescue content of M38-G.
Past redemption of Abraham grounding present promise that replaces shame with dignity is ransomed-identity gratitude and memory, fitting M38-G.
Job's framing of ransom as deliverance from threatening adversary through divine intervention fits M38-G's personal-ransom and soul-cry content better than M38-B's panic-in-mortal-danger register.
Corporate pleading for divine ransom from troubles with ransomed-identity vocabulary fits M38-G's community-ransom and distress-calling-for-redemption content.
Community urgently pleading for redemption from shame and defeat appealing to steadfast love is ransomed-identity hope calling for divine rescue, fitting M38-G.
Confidence that God will ransom the soul from Sheol where humans cannot expresses eschatological ransomed trust, fitting M38-G's soul-deliverance and trust content.
God redeeming the soul safely from battle enemies fits M38-G's personal-ransom and soul-delivered content more directly than M38-B's group-panic register.
Ransom from human oppression freeing the will for obedience names personal redemptive rescue enabling covenant response, fitting M38-G.
God ransoming Israel from all iniquities as total eschatological redemption covering every sin fits M38-G's comprehensive ransom and soul-hope content.
The ransomed returning with everlasting joy replacing sorrow is the joy-of-the-ransomed-state transformation that defines M38-G.
Ransomed of the Lord returning crowned with everlasting joy with sorrow displaced is the paradigmatic ransomed-identity-joy content of M38-G.
God personally promising to ransom the prophet from violent oppressors fits M38-G's personal covenantal ransom content rather than M38-B's group-panic register.
God ransoming his people from overpowering enemies as accomplished covenant rescue fits the ransomed-identity and delivered-by-God content of M38-G.
Ransom offered but refused by a will turned away in deceptive rebellion engages the ransomed-identity framework negatively, fitting M38-G's ransom-and-will content.
God announcing ransom from Sheol and Death itself as ultimate saving act fits M38-G's ransomed-from-death and eschatological-deliverance content.
The people ransoming Jonathan from a death sentence in communal solidarity fits M38-G's ransomed-life-preserved content over M38-B's panic-in-mortal-danger register.
God ransoming from famine and war as mortal dangers fits M38-G's divine-ransom-from-death content; the pa.dah vocabulary and divine-rescue framing align with M38-G.
God atoning for overwhelming transgressions, clearing guilt the inner being cannot bear, is the conscience-cleansed-through-atonement content that defines M38-D.
Salvation defined as rescue from hostile enemies with corporate safety and vindication from threat fits the physical-rescue-from-danger register of M38-B.
Salvation originating from the Jews naming the covenantal-historical source of redemption fits M38-A's received eschatological salvation grounded in particular revelation.
Paul expecting personal deliverance through prayer and Spirit's aid with the inner being sustained by hope fits M38-F's hope-sustained-toward-not-yet-fully-received rescue.
Divine appointment to obtain salvation contrasted with wrath, replacing dread with assured rescue, names received eschatological salvation through election, fitting M38-A.
Confidence that readers possess things belonging to salvation with assured hope grounded in their trajectory fits M38-F's hope-sustained and anticipatory posture.
Noah's faith-driven ark construction saving his household from physical destruction is faith producing embodied action delivering from physical danger, fitting M38-B.
God named as the speaker's present salvation replacing fear with trust and joy in experienced deliverance fits M38-G's ye.shu.ah salvation-arrived gratitude content.
Drawing water with joy from salvation's wells is glad active reception of delivered life expressing gratitude, fitting M38-G's joy-of-the-ransomed content.
Patient waiting vindicated when salvation arrives transforming the soul into exultant joy fits M38-G's salvation-arrived-and-rejoiced-over content rather than M38-F's still-waiting posture.
The messenger proclaiming salvation as beautiful news with joyful reception fits M38-G's salvation-arrived-and-received-with-joy content.
Job's confidence that his willingness to approach God will be his salvation grounds hope in integrity before God, fitting M38-F's hope-sustained posture toward not-yet-secured salvation.
Firstborn sons ransomed through substitutionary sacrifice echoing covenant-redemption from Egypt with gratitude enacted in ongoing ritual fits M38-G's ransomed-identity memory and gratitude content.
Israel's redeemed identity invoked as basis for appealing for atonement of blood guilt fits M38-G's ransomed-identity grounding relational standing before God.
Memory of redemption from Egypt commanding just treatment of the vulnerable names gratitude-motivating-will, the hallmark of M38-G.
God going out to redeem Israel defining the people's identity and relational bond fits M38-G's ransomed-identity and covenant-forming content.
God's act of redeeming Israel constituting the people's identity and his glory fits M38-G's ransomed-identity and covenant-forming content.
No human able to ransom another from death framing human inability over ultimate mortality fits M38-G's ransom vocabulary and soul-imperilled content.
Zion's redemption through justice requiring repentance of the inner being for the ransom to take effect fits M38-G's ransomed-identity and will-turning-toward-rescue content.
